title: Senior AI Engineer – Internal Tools & Platform
company_stage: Growth/Scale-up (recognized as market leader with 20M+ students, 20K+ schools, EdTech Top 40)
company_focus:
AI-powered student safety and wellness solutions for K-12
schools
description:
About Securly

Tech Giant Scale. EdTech Innovation. Unmatched Student
Impact.

Securly is the market leader in AI-powered student safety
and wellness solutions, trusted by more than 20 million
students across 20,000+ schools worldwide. Our mission is to
help schools create safer, more supportive learning
environments for every student.

Since launching the world’s first cloud-based web filter for
K–12 in 2013, Securly has continued to set the standard for
student well-being technology. Our platform combines AI and
human insight to proactively identify and respond to risks
such as bullying, self-harm, and violence—technology
credited with preventing 2,000+ potential tragedies.

Securly is recognized as an EdTech Top 40 “Most Used
Product”, reflecting broad adoption and daily reliance by
schools nationwide. We are also proud to be named to the
2026 GSV 150, recognizing the world’s most transformational
companies in digital learning for innovation, scale, and
real-world impact.

Our people-first culture is backed by strong engagement:
- 94% of employees are proud to work here
- 91% rate manager effectiveness above benchmarks

At Securly, we don’t just build products—we protect
students, partner with educators, and build trust at scale.

About the Role

We are seeking a Senior AI Engineer – Internal Tools &
Systems to design, build, and operate production-grade, AI-
powered internal tools that increase organizational
efficiency and reduce manual effort across teams such as
Finance, Sales, Customer Success, Support, and Product.

This role is centered on practical, applied AI. You will
build systems that are reliable, adopted, and embedded
directly into day-to-day workflows—not experiments or proofs
of concept. You will own solutions end-to-end, from problem
definition through deployment and iteration, working closely
with leadership and cross-functional stakeholders.

This is a senior individual contributor role for engineers
who enjoy building full-stack internal systems and applying
AI responsibly to deliver meaningful business impact.

Location: Pune, Maharashtra, India Work Type: Hybrid (onsite
2 days per week) Compensation: ₹48–64 LPA (based on
experience and demonstrated capability)



What Success Looks Like

- Internal AI tools meaningfully improve how teams work by
  increasing efficiency and reducing cycle time.
- AI systems are production-ready, reliable, and trusted for
  real operational use.
- Tools see sustained, repeat usage across multiple business
  units.
- Clear, repeatable patterns exist for evaluating, building,
  and deploying internal AI solutions.
- Stakeholders have clarity on priorities, timelines, and
  tradeoffs.
- Impact is demonstrated through outcomes and adoption—not
  just features shipped.

What You’ll Accomplish in Your First 12 Months

First 90 Days

- Build a strong understanding of internal workflows, data
  sources, and operational pain points.
- Review existing internal tools, automation, and prior AI
  initiatives.
- Deliver early improvements or prototypes such as AI-assisted
  search, summarization, or workflow support.

First 180 Days

- Design and launch production-ready internal AI tools that
  reduce manual effort or cycle time.
- Improve reliability, usability, and adoption of AI-enabled
  systems.
- Establish responsible patterns for building, validating, and
  deploying AI features.

First 360 Days

- Own multiple internal AI systems used regularly across
  teams.
- Drive adoption, iteration, and long-term maintenance.
- Contribute to internal standards and best practices for AI-
  enabled tools.

Core ResponsibilitiesInternal Tools & AI Systems Development

- Design and build internal tools used daily across the
  organization.
- Develop AI-powered capabilities such as internal search,
  copilots, summarization, classification, and workflow
  automation.
- Ship full-stack applications using modern frontend
  frameworks and cloud-native, serverless backend
  infrastructure.
- Ensure systems are production-ready, reliable, and easy to
  operate.

Applied AI & Automation

- Apply LLMs and automation techniques to real operational
  problems.
- Evaluate tradeoffs between accuracy, cost, latency, and
  usability.
- Integrate AI systems with internal data sources and third-
  party platforms.

Platform, Infrastructure & Reliability

- Build scalable APIs and services using AWS and serverless
  architectures.
- Implement data pipelines and background processing using
  services such as S3, SQS, or similar.
- Ensure observability, logging, and operational stability.

Collaboration & Ownership

- Partner with Finance, Enablement, Sales, Support, Product,
  and Engineering teams.
- Own features end-to-end, from design through rollout and
  iteration.
- Communicate clearly about scope, priorities, tradeoffs, and
  outcomes.

Core Technology Environment

- Frontend: TypeScript/JavaScript, Next.js (App Router),
  JSX/TSX, Tailwind CSS, Radix UI, v0 (Vercel)
- Backend: TypeScript/Node.js, serverless APIs, lightweight
  frameworks
- Cloud: AWS Lambda, S3, SQS
- Data: PostgreSQL, ORM-based access patterns, vector search
- AI: LLM integration, embeddings, RAG-style internal tools

Required Qualifications

- Experience building and operating production software
  systems applying AI or automation (typically developed
  through 5+ years of hands-on engineering experience).
- Strong experience building full-stack systems, including
  modern React-based frontends and cloud-native backends.
- Proficiency in TypeScript/JavaScript and modern React
  frameworks (Next.js, JSX/TSX, Tailwind CSS).
- Experience applying LLMs or AI-assisted workflows in
  practical internal or user-facing systems.
- Experience owning features end-to-end, including deployment
  and ongoing support.
- Demonstrated judgment around reliability, maintainability,
  and user adoption.
- Experience working effectively across time zones and with
  cross-functional stakeholders.

Preferred Qualifications

- Experience building internal tools for non-engineering
  teams.
- Familiarity with AWS serverless services (Lambda, S3, SQS).
- Exposure to vector search, embeddings, RAG-style systems, or
  AI-enhanced search.
- Experience integrating with platforms such as Salesforce,
  ticketing, or analytics tools.
- Prior experience operating at a Senior or equivalent IC
  level.

Why Securly

- Engineering with Impact: Build internal systems that
  directly improve how teams operate.
- Practical AI at Scale: Work on real, production AI systems.
- Career Growth: Expand ownership with pathways toward Staff-
  level impact.
- Collaborative Culture: Partner closely with teams in India
  and the U.S.

Benefits

- Hybrid Work Model
- Comprehensive Health Insurance (employee, spouse, children)
- Acidental & Term Life Insurance
- Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
- Learning & Development reimbursement
- Paid Time Off
- Public Holidays (12+ per year)
- Retirement Benefits (EPF & gratuity)
- Equipment & WFH setup support
- Parental Leave (as per statutory norms)

Equal Opportunity Employer

Securly is an Equal Opportunity Employer committed to
inclusion, fairness, and respect. We welcome applicants from
all backgrounds, identities, and experiences.  #LI-REMOTE
#LI-DO1

ai_classification_reasoning:
This role involves directly building AI-powered internal
tools including LLM integration, embeddings, and RAG-style
systems. The engineer will design, develop, and deploy
production AI capabilities like copilots, summarization,
classification, and workflow automation - not just
supporting infrastructure for others' AI work.
